Explanation: Marta Minujín is a prominent performance and conceptual artist, listed among the main exponents of performance art [27906]. She is known for large-scale participatory works. The "Parthenon of Books" is a major artwork by Minujín, originally created in 1983 in Buenos Aires, which was destroyed after the end of the dictatorship. It was reconstructed in 2017 in Kassel, Germany. The timeline and context fit the clues: Minujín was born in 1943 (within the 1920–1940 range), is the eldest of four children, and has received major awards in the 2010s. The destroyed and reconstructed work is the "Parthenon of Books."
Exact Answer: Parthenon of Books
Confidence: 95%
